fact: January 24 is exactly 24 days into the year - what are the odds of that perfect match?
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are typically burning through their winter fat stores around this time, which can make them look extra fluffy!
The sun is still rising later each morning in most places, even though days have been getting longer since December 21st - weird, right?
If you're born on January 24, you're right on the edge between Aquarius and Capricorn in astrology!
In Antarctica, this is peak summer and the sun never sets, making it bright as day at midnight!
Tree sap in cold climates is typically at its thickest around this time because it's loaded with natural antifreeze chemicals!
The moon often looks extra bright and crisp in January skies because cold air holds less water vapor to blur your view!
Many hibernating animals like bears are in their deepest sleep phase right now - their heart rate can drop to just 8 beats per minute!
In places like Canada and Alaska, your breath can freeze and fall to the ground as ice crystals on days like this!
January 24 falls exactly one month and three days after the winter solstice, making it a middle point in winter's grip!
Evergreen trees are actually working overtime right now in northern climates, doing photosynthesis in freezing temperatures while other plants sleep!
In the Southern Hemisphere, this date is when many nocturnal animals typically start staying awake longer because the nights are getting shorter!
